CalifOrnia
Fair P01itical
Practices Commission
Decenber 6, 799L
Honorabl-e Charles Egan coff
2 Marcela Ave.
San Francisco, CA 94l"L6
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-501
Dear Judge Goff:
You have requested advj-ce concerning your reporting
obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act.1/
OUESTION
As a retired judge who has been serving on tenporary
assignments, are you required to file Statements of Economic
fnterests (Form 721-)? If so, when are the statements due?
CONCI.USION
Once you have served 30 days or more in any 12-nonth period,
you must begin filing 
statements of econonic interests with the
clerk of the court to whj,ch you have been assigned. You then must
file annual statements until your assignment ends. Once your
assignment ends and you do not receive a new assignnent, you must
file a leaving office statenent within 30 days of leavj-ng your
l-ast assignnent.
'/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. AlI statutory references
are to the Governnent Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations Section
18000, et ses. AII references to regulations are to Title 
2
Division 6 of the California code of Regu)-ations.
428 J Street, Suite SOtl . P.O. Box t3O7 o Sircramenro CA 958114-OUO7 o 1916'1122-5660


Judge Charles coff
Page 2
FACTA
You retired from San Francisco Municipal- Court on June 4,
1990, and filed a teaving office statement. Since JuIy 9, 1991,
you have received assignrnents to various courts.
ANALYAIS
The Act requires that judges f il-e statements of economic
interests (Forn 721). (Section A72OO-a72O4.) However, ReguLation
l8724zl provides that retired judges are not required to file
statements of economic interests unless they serve or expect to
serve 30 days or more in any L2-month period.
If you have served 30 days or more j.n any 12-month period
since the date of your retirenent, you rnust fite an assurning
office statement of economic interests (Form 721). The [Date
Assuned Officerr as indicated on the cover page of the Form 721
should be the date on which you served a total of 30 days since
your retirernent. The assuming office Forn 721 must be filed no
Iater than 30 days after the date you determine to be your rrdate
assumed off ice. r' If the filing deadline has passed, please file
your statenent as soon as possibl-e, and attach a letter explaj.ning
vrhy the statement is late. 
The assuming office Form 721 should be
filed with the clerk of the court to which you were assigned on
the date you determine to be your rtdate assumed off ice.rr The
clerk of the court wj.lI nake and retain a copy and forr.rard the
originaf statement to the Fair Political Practices Commission.
After filing the assuning office statement, you nust file
rrannualrr statements no l-ater than March 1 of each year until you
are no longer accepting assignments. At the tirne of your 1ast
assignnent, and $rhen you do not anticipate accepting assignrnents
again, you must file a rrleaving office'r statement. The leaving
office statement must be filed within 30 days of the termination
of your assignment. Each of your required statements should be
filed with the clerk of the court to which you are assigned on the
date the statement is due.
)l-'copies of Commission regulations and opinions are
nany lar^, Iibraries. 
Alternatively, copies of these
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the
cost of 10C per page.
available in
nateriaLs and
Cornniss ion at a


Judge charles Goff
page 3
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact ne at (916) 322-5662-
Sincerely,
Scott HaIIabrin
Acting General- counsel
帥
″
by: colleen l'lccee
Pol- it ica I Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance and
Analysi.s Division
